title: Collection and preparation of colon from mice to calculate the colon index and for immunohistochemistry
author: Amandine EVEN

steps_raw.instruction: The mouse is weighed, which is necessary for subsequent calculation of the colon index.
If present, feces are carefully removed. To do this, gently slide a forceps along the colon from the proximal to the distal part to expel the feces.
After an appropriate euthanasia, the caecum and the colon are dissected from the mouse. The colon corresponds to the section between the cecum and the anus.
The index is calculated as the colon mass (mg)/mouse body mass (g).
Colons are sectioned in two with a scalpel blade - proximal portion, from the part which followed the caecum to the middle - distal portion from the middle to the part which was connected to the anus
Colon sections are maintained in a petri dish full of cold 1X PBS.
For futur analyses of colon RNA or protein , sections are directly transfered into dry 2 mL tubes and transfered on dry ice. After the collection of all samples, the tubes are transfered to -80 °C .

With a 10 mL syringe and a 21G needle, flush the inside of the colon with cold 1X PBS until it is completely clean. Be careful not to damage the tissues. Apply a steady flow of cold PBS to distend the colon and avoid touching the tissue with the needle.
For subsequent colon immunohistopathology or immunohistochemistry , tissues need to be fixed.
For immunohistopathology, sections are transfered into 2 mL tubes containing cold 4% PFA and are kept at 4 °C 1-2 days before embedding.
For immunohistochemistry, sections are directly inserted into a mold and transfered into a bottle of cold formalin. Bottle are kept at 4 °C 1-2 days before embedding.
The colon is weighed with a precision scale.
The lenght of the colon is mesured with a ruler from the caecum to the end. A photography is done with the ruler for record.
Excess 1X PBS is dried by very quickly applying the section to a Kimwipe and the tissue is stored.
